observations.summary: Aviel's advice to 'pick up a stone and give it a name' as a way to seek answers from a tangible power parallels the thematic motif of seeking concrete sources of power or truth in the narrative.

source_evidence.quote: Three nights ago Aviel had hissed across their fire: "If you are still searching for your source, boy, pick up a stone and give it a name. Seek answers from a power you can touch."

observations.target_hint: This echoes the broader narrative theme of Dan's quest for understanding and connection to a tangible source of power or truth, as seen in his journey and interactions with physical and metaphysical elements.
